description_long: This is a classic bolt-action rifle with a wooden stock and a long, slender metal barrel. The stock is made from polished wood, extending from the butt to the fore-end, providing a sturdy grip and shoulder support. The receiver and barrel are constructed from blued or parkerized steel, with visible iron sights mounted on top for aiming. The bolt handle protrudes from the right side, indicating a manual cycling action for chambering rounds. The rifle features an internal magazine, likely loaded from the top, and a simple trigger guard beneath the action. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of military surplus or hunting rifles from the early to mid-20th century.
description: A classic bolt-action rifle with a wooden stock, long metal barrel, and iron sights. The design is robust and utilitarian, featuring a protruding bolt handle and internal magazine.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, with the wooden stock, metal barrel, and bolt handle clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the narrow profile of the barrel and the front sight post.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the rifle's length, wooden stock, and the absence of the bolt handle on this side.
description_view_3: Rear-on view showing the butt of the stock and the alignment of the barrel and sights.
